questions.question_text: A school has $850$ students. $64\%$ travel by bus. How many students travel by bus?

options.why: Found the $36\%$ who do not travel by bus instead of the $64\%$ who do.
Converted $64\%$ to $0.064$ instead of $0.64$, misplacing the decimal point.

questions.question_text: Which is greater: $15\%$ of $80$, or $80\%$ of $15$?

options.text: $15\%$ of $80$, because it is taken from the larger quantity $80$.
options.why: Assumed a larger base always wins, ignoring that the smaller percentage offsets it.

options.text: $80\%$ of $15$, because $80\%$ is the larger percentage.
options.why: Assumed a larger percentage always wins, ignoring that it acts on the smaller base.
